v.CNV: 神的儿子（有些抄本无“　神的儿子”一句）耶稣基督福音的开始。
v.ESV: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
v.GAE: 하나님의 아들 예수 그리스도의 복음의 시작이라
v.KJV: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God;
v.NASB: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
v.NIV: The beginning of the good news about Jesus the Messiah, the Son of God,
v.NRSV: The beginning of the good news of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
v.SAENEW: 하나님의 아들 예수 그리스도의 복음의 시작은 이러하다.
v.WLB: 하나님의 아들 예수 그리스도에 관한 복음은 이렇게 시작됩니다.

v.CNV: 正如以赛亚先知的书上写着：“看哪，我差遣我的使者在你面前，预备你的道路；
v.ESV: As it is written in Isaiah the prophet, “Behold, I send my messenger before your face, who will prepare your way,
v.GAE: 선지자 이사야의 글에 보라 내가 내 사자를 네 앞에 보내노니 그가 네 길을 준비하리라
v.KJV: As it is written in the prophets, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
v.NASB: As it is written in Isaiah the prophet: 'BEHOLD, I SEND MY MESSENGER AHEAD OF YOU, WHO WILL PREPARE YOUR WAY;
v.NIV: as it is written in Isaiah the prophet: “I will send my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way” —
v.NRSV: As it is written in the prophet Isaiah, “See, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way;
v.SAENEW: 예언자 이사야의 글에 기록하기를, "보아라, 내가 내 심부름꾼을 너보다 앞서 보낸다. 그가 네 길을 닦을 것이다."
v.WLB: 예언자 이사야의 글에 “내가 네 앞에 내 심부름꾼을 보낼 것이다. 그가 네 길을 준비할 것이다.”

v.CNV: 在旷野有呼喊者的声音：‘预备主的道，修直他的路！’”
v.ESV: the voice of one crying in the wilderness: ‘Prepare the way of the Lord, make his paths straight,’”
v.GAE: 광야에 외치는 자의 소리가 있어 이르되 너희는 주의 길을 준비하라 그의 오실 길을 곧게 하라 기록된 것과 같이
v.KJV: The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
v.NASB: THE VOICE OF ONE CRYING IN THE WILDERNESS, 'MAKE READY THE WAY OF THE LORD, MAKE HIS PATHS STRAIGHT.''
v.NIV: “a voice of one calling in the wilderness, ‘Prepare the way for the Lord, make straight paths for him.’”
v.NRSV: the voice of one crying out in the wilderness: ‘Prepare the way of the Lord, make his paths straight,’”
v.SAENEW: "광야에서 외치는 이의 소리가 있다. '너희는 주님의 길을 예비하고, 그의 길을 곧게 하여라'" 한 것과 같이,
v.WLB: “광야에서 외치는 사람의 소리가 있다. ‘주를 위해 길을 예비하라. 그분을 위해 길을 곧게 하라’” 고 기록돼 있는 대로

v.CNV: 照这话，施洗的约翰在旷野出现了，传讲悔改的洗礼，使罪得赦。
v.ESV: John appeared, baptizing in the wilderness and proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
v.GAE: 세례 요한이 광야에 이르러 죄 사함을 받게 하는 회개의 세례를 전파하니
v.KJV: John did baptize in the wilderness, and preach the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins.
v.NASB: John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
v.NIV: And so John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness, preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
v.NRSV: John the baptizer appeared in the wilderness, proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
v.SAENEW: 세례자 요한이 광야에 나타나서, 죄를 용서받게 하는 회개의 세례를 선포하였다.
v.WLB: 세례자 요한이 광야에 나타나서 죄 용서를 위한 회개의 세례를 선포했습니다.

v.CNV: 犹太全地和全耶路撒冷的人，都出来到他那里去，承认自己的罪，在约旦河里受了他的洗。
v.ESV: And all the country of Judea and all Jerusalem were going out to him and were being baptized by him in the river Jordan, confessing their sins.
v.GAE: 온 유대 지방과 예루살렘 사람이 다 나아가 자기 죄를 자복하고 요단 강에서 그에게 세례를 받더라
v.KJV: And there went out unto him all the land of Judaea, and they of Jerusalem, and were all baptized of him in the river of Jordan, confessing their sins.
v.NASB: And all the country of Judea was going out to him, and all the people of Jerusalem; and they were being baptized by him in the Jordan River, confessing their sins.
v.NIV: The whole Judean countryside and all the people of Jerusalem went out to him. Confessing their sins, they were baptized by him in the Jordan River.
v.NRSV: And people from the whole Judean countryside and all the people of Jerusalem were going out to him, and were baptized by him in the river Jordan, confessing their sins.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 온 유대 지방 사람들과 온 예루살렘 주민들이 그에게로 나아가서, 자기들의 죄를 고백하며, 요단 강에서 그에게 세례를 받았다.
v.WLB: 유대 온 지방과 예루살렘 모든 사람들이 요한에게 나아와 자기 죄를 고백하고 요단 강에서 요한에게 세례를 받았습니다.

v.CNV: 约翰身穿骆驼毛的衣服，腰束皮带，吃的是蝗虫和野蜜。
v.ESV: Now John was clothed with camel's hair and wore a leather belt around his waist and ate locusts and wild honey.
v.GAE: 요한은 낙타털 옷을 입고 허리에 가죽 띠를 띠고 메뚜기와 석청을 먹더라
v.KJV: And John was clothed with camel’s hair, and with a girdle of a skin about his loins; and he did eat locusts and wild honey;
v.NASB: John was clothed with camel's hair and wore a leather belt around his waist, and his diet was locusts and wild honey.
v.NIV: John wore clothing made of camel’s hair, with a leather belt around his waist, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
v.NRSV: Now John was clothed with camel’s hair, with a leather belt around his waist, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
v.SAENEW: 요한은 낙타 털옷을 입고, 허리에 가죽 띠를 띠고, 메뚜기와 들꿀을 먹고 살았다.
v.WLB: 요한은 낙타털로 만든 옷을 입고 허리에 가죽띠를 두르고 메뚜기와 들꿀을 먹었습니다.

v.CNV: 他传讲说：“有一位在我以后来的，能力比我大，我就是弯腰给他解鞋带都没有资格。
v.ESV: And he preached, saying, “After me comes he who is mightier than I, the strap of whose sandals I am not worthy to stoop down and untie.
v.GAE: 그가 전파하여 이르되 나보다 능력 많으신 이가 내 뒤에 오시나니 나는 굽혀 그의 신발끈을 풀기도 감당하지 못하겠노라
v.KJV: And preached, saying, There cometh one mightier than I after me, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to stoop down and unloose.
v.NASB: And he was preaching, and saying, 'After me One is coming who is mightier than I, and I am not fit to stoop down and untie the thong of His sandals.
v.NIV: And this was his message: “After me comes the one more powerful than I, the straps of whose sandals I am not worthy to stoop down and untie.
v.NRSV: He proclaimed, “The one who is more powerful than I is coming after me; I am not worthy to stoop down and untie the thong of his sandals.
v.SAENEW: 그는 이렇게 선포하였다. "나보다 더 능력이 있는 이가 내 뒤에 오십니다. 나는 몸을 굽혀서 그의 신발 끈을 풀 자격조차 없습니다.
v.WLB: 그리고 요한은 이렇게 선포했습니다. “나보다 더 능력 있는 분이 내 뒤에 오실 텐데 나는 몸을 굽혀 그분의 신발끈을 풀 자격도 없다.

v.CNV: 我用水给你们施洗，他却要用圣灵给你们施洗。”
v.ESV: I have baptized you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.”
v.GAE: 나는 너희에게 물로 세례를 베풀었거니와 그는 너희에게 성령으로 세례를 베푸시리라
v.KJV: I indeed have baptized you with water: but he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.
v.NASB: 'I baptized you with water; but He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.'
v.NIV: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.”
v.NRSV: I have baptized you with water; but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.”
v.SAENEW: 나는 여러분에게 물로 세례를 주었지만, 그는 여러분에게 성령으로 세례를 주실 것입니다."
v.WLB: 나는 너희에게 물로 세례를 주지만 그분은 너희에게 성령으로 세례를 주실 것이다.”

v.CNV: 那时候耶稣从加利利的拿撒勒来，在约旦河里受了约翰的洗。
v.ESV: In those days Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
v.GAE: 그 때에 예수께서 갈릴리 나사렛으로부터 와서 요단 강에서 요한에게 세례를 받으시고
v.KJV: And it came to pass in those days, that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized of John in Jordan.
v.NASB: In those days Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
v.NIV: At that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
v.NRSV: In those days Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
v.SAENEW: 그 무렵에 예수께서 갈릴리 나사렛으로부터 오셔서, 요단 강에서 요한에게 세례를 받으셨다.
v.WLB: 그 무렵에 예수께서 갈릴리 나사렛에서 요단 강으로 오셔서 요한에게 세례를 받으셨습니다.

v.CNV: 他从水里一上来，就看见天裂开了，圣灵仿佛鸽子降在他身上。
v.ESV: And when he came up out of the water, immediately he saw the heavens being torn open and the Spirit descending on him like a dove.
v.GAE: 곧 물에서 올라오실새 하늘이 갈라짐과 성령이 비둘기 같이 자기에게 내려오심을 보시더니
v.KJV: And straightway coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens opened, and the Spirit like a dove descending upon him:
v.NASB: Immediately coming up out of the water, He saw the heavens opening, and the Spirit like a dove descending upon Him;
v.NIV: Just as Jesus was coming up out of the water, he saw heaven being torn open and the Spirit descending on him like a dove.
v.NRSV: And just as he was coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens torn apart and the Spirit descending like a dove on him.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 물 속에서 막 올라오시는데, 하늘이 갈라지고, 성령이 비둘기같이 자기에게 내려오는 것을 보셨다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 물에서 막 나오실 때 하늘이 열리고 성령이 비둘기처럼 자기에게 내려오는 것을 보셨습니다.

v.CNV: 又有声音从天上来说：“你是我的爱子，我喜悦你。”
v.ESV: And a voice came from heaven, “You are my beloved Son; with you I am well pleased.”
v.GAE: 하늘로부터 소리가 나기를 너는 내 사랑하는 아들이라 내가 너를 기뻐하노라 하시니라
v.KJV: And there came a voice from heaven, saying, Thou art my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.
v.NASB: and a voice came out of the heavens: 'You are My beloved Son, in You I am well-pleased.'
v.NIV: And a voice came from heaven: “You are my Son, whom I love; with you I am well pleased.”
v.NRSV: And a voice came from heaven, “You are my Son, the Beloved; with you I am well pleased.”
v.SAENEW: 그리고 하늘로부터 소리가 났다. "너는 내 사랑하는 아들이다. 내가 너를 좋아한다."
v.WLB: 그리고 하늘에서 소리가 들려왔습니다. “너는 내가 사랑하는 아들이다. 내가 너를 무척 기뻐한다.”

v.CNV: 圣灵随即催促耶稣到旷野去。
v.ESV: The Spirit immediately drove him out into the wilderness.
v.GAE: 성령이 곧 예수를 광야로 몰아내신지라
v.KJV: And immediately the Spirit driveth him into the wilderness.
v.NASB: Immediately the Spirit impelled Him to go out into the wilderness.
v.NIV: At once the Spirit sent him out into the wilderness,
v.NRSV: And the Spirit immediately drove him out into the wilderness.
v.SAENEW: 그리고 곧 성령이 예수를 광야로 내보내셨다.
v.WLB: 그러고 나서 곧 성령이 예수를 광야로 내보내셨습니다.

v.CNV: 他在那里四十天，受撒但的试探，和野兽在一起，有天使来服事他。
v.ESV: And he was in the wilderness forty days, being tempted by Satan. And he was with the wild animals, and the angels were ministering to him.
v.GAE: 광야에서 사십 일을 계시면서 사탄에게 시험을 받으시며 들짐승과 함께 계시니 천사들이 수종들더라
v.KJV: And he was there in the wilderness forty days, tempted of Satan; and was with the wild beasts; and the angels ministered unto him.
v.NASB: And He was in the wilderness forty days being tempted by Satan; and He was with the wild beasts, and the angels were ministering to Him.
v.NIV: and he was in the wilderness forty days, being tempted by Satan. He was with the wild animals, and angels attended him.
v.NRSV: He was in the wilderness forty days, tempted by Satan; and he was with the wild beasts; and the angels waited on him.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 사십 일 동안 광야에 계셨는데, 거기서 사탄에게 시험을 받으셨다. 예수께서 들짐승들과 함께 지내셨는데, 천사들이 그의 시중을 들었다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 40일 동안 광야에 계시면서 사탄에게 시험을 받으셨습니다. 그때 예수께서 들짐승들과 함께 계셨는데 천사들이 예수를 시중들었습니다.

v.CNV: 约翰被捕以后，耶稣来到加利利，宣讲　神的福音，
v.ESV: Now after John was arrested, Jesus came into Galilee, proclaiming the gospel of God,
v.GAE: 요한이 잡힌 후 예수께서 갈릴리에 오셔서 하나님의 복음을 전파하여
v.KJV: Now after that John was put in prison, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the gospel of the kingdom of God,
v.NASB: Now after John had been taken into custody, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the gospel of God,
v.NIV: After John was put in prison, Jesus went into Galilee, proclaiming the good news of God.
v.NRSV: Now after John was arrested, Jesus came to Galilee, proclaiming the good news of God,
v.SAENEW: 요한이 잡힌 뒤에, 예수께서 갈릴리에 오셔서, 하나님의 복음을 선포하셨다.
v.WLB: 요한이 감옥에 갇힌 뒤 예수께서는 갈릴리로 가셔서 하나님의 복음을 선포하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 说：“时候到了，　神的国近了，你们应当悔改，相信福音。”
v.ESV: and saying, “The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand; repent and believe in the gospel.”
v.GAE: 이르시되 때가 찼고 하나님의 나라가 가까이 왔으니 회개하고 복음을 믿으라 하시더라
v.KJV: And saying, The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand: repent ye, and believe the gospel.
v.NASB: and saying, 'The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand; repent and believe in the gospel.'
v.NIV: “The time has come,” he said. “The kingdom of God has come near. Repent and believe the good news!”
v.NRSV: and saying, “The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God has come near; repent, and believe in the good news.”
v.SAENEW: "때가 찼다. 하나님의 나라가 가까이 왔다. 회개하여라. 복음을 믿어라."
v.WLB: “때가 찼고 하나님 나라가 가까이 왔으니 회개하고 복음을 믿으라!”

v.CNV: 耶稣沿着加利利海边行走，看见西门和他弟弟安得烈在海上撒网；他们是渔夫。
v.ESV: Passing alongside the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew the brother of Simon casting a net into the sea, for they were fishermen.
v.GAE: 갈릴리 해변으로 지나가시다가 시몬과 그 형제 안드레가 바다에 그물 던지는 것을 보시니 그들은 어부라
v.KJV: Now as he walked by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew his brother casting a net into the sea: for they were fishers.
v.NASB: As He was going along by the Sea of Galilee, He saw Simon and Andrew, the brother of Simon, casting a net in the sea; for they were fishermen.
v.NIV: As Jesus walked beside the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew casting a net into the lake, for they were fishermen.
v.NRSV: As Jesus passed along the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew casting a net into the sea — for they were fishermen.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 갈릴리 바닷가를 지나가시다가, 시몬과 그의 동생 안드레가 바다에서 그물을 던지고 있는 것을 보셨다. 그들은 어부였다.
v.WLB: 그리고 예수께서 갈릴리 호숫가를 거닐다가 시몬과 그 동생 안드레가 호수에 그물을 던지는 것을 보셨는데 그들은 어부였습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣就对他们说：“来跟从我，我要使你们成为得人的渔夫。”
v.ESV: And Jesus said to them, “Follow me, and I will make you become fishers of men.”
v.GAE: 예수께서 이르시되 나를 따라오라 내가 너희로 사람을 낚는 어부가 되게 하리라 하시니
v.KJV: And Jesus said unto them, Come ye after me, and I will make you to become fishers of men.
v.NASB: And Jesus said to them, 'Follow Me, and I will make you become fishers of men.'
v.NIV: “Come, follow me,” Jesus said, “and I will send you out to fish for people.”
v.NRSV: And Jesus said to them, “Follow me and I will make you fish for people.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "나를 따라오너라. 내가 너희를 사람을 낚는 어부가 되게 하겠다."
v.WLB: 예수께서 말씀하셨습니다. “나를 따르라. 내가 너희를 사람 낚는 어부가 되게 하겠다.”

v.CNV: 他们立刻撇下网，跟从了他。
v.ESV: And immediately they left their nets and followed him.
v.GAE: 곧 그물을 버려 두고 따르니라
v.KJV: And straightway they forsook their nets, and followed him.
v.NASB: Immediately they left their nets and followed Him.
v.NIV: At once they left their nets and followed him.
v.NRSV: And immediately they left their nets and followed him.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 곧 그물을 버리고 예수를 따라갔다.
v.WLB: 시몬과 안드레는 곧 그물을 버려 두고 예수를 따랐습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣稍往前走，看见西庇太的儿子雅各，和雅各的弟弟约翰，正在船上整理鱼网，
v.ESV: And going on a little farther, he saw James the son of Zebedee and John his brother, who were in their boat mending the nets.
v.GAE: 조금 더 가시다가 세베대의 아들 야고보와 그 형제 요한을 보시니 그들도 배에 있어 그물을 깁는데
v.KJV: And when he had gone a little farther thence, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, who also were in the ship mending their nets.
v.NASB: Going on a little farther, He saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, who were also in the boat mending the nets.
v.NIV: When he had gone a little farther, he saw James son of Zebedee and his brother John in a boat, preparing their nets.
v.NRSV: As he went a little farther, he saw James son of Zebedee and his brother John, who were in their boat mending the nets.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 조금 더 가시다가, 세베대의 아들 야고보와 그의 동생 요한이 배에서 그물을 깁고 있는 것을 보시고,
v.WLB: 예수께서 조금 더 가시다가 세베대의 아들 야고보와 그 동생 요한이 배에서 그물을 깁고 있는 것을 보시고

v.CNV: 他立即呼召他们。他们撇下父亲西庇太和雇工在船上，就跟从他去了。
v.ESV: And immediately he called them, and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired servants and followed him.
v.GAE: 곧 부르시니 그 아버지 세베대를 품꾼들과 함께 배에 버려 두고 예수를 따라가니라
v.KJV: And straightway he called them: and they left their father Zebedee in the ship with the hired servants, and went after him.
v.NASB: Immediately He called them; and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired servants, and went away to follow Him.
v.NIV: Without delay he called them, and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired men and followed him.
v.NRSV: Immediately he called them; and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired men, and followed him.
v.SAENEW: 곧바로 그들을 부르셨다. 그들은 아버지 세베대를 일꾼들과 함께 배에 남겨 두고, 곧 예수를 따라갔다.
v.WLB: 곧 그들을 부르셨습니다. 그러자 그들도 아버지 세베대와 일꾼들을 배에 남겨 두고 곧바로 예수를 따랐습니다.

v.CNV: 他们到了迦百农，耶稣随即在安息日进入会堂教导人。
v.ESV: And they went into Capernaum, and immediately on the Sabbath he entered the synagogue and was teaching.
v.GAE: 그들이 가버나움에 들어가니라 예수께서 곧 안식일에 회당에 들어가 가르치시매
v.KJV: And they went into Capernaum; and straightway on the sabbath day he entered into the synagogue, and taught.
v.NASB: They went into Capernaum; and immediately on the Sabbath He entered the synagogue and began to teach.
v.NIV: They went to Capernaum, and when the Sabbath came, Jesus went into the synagogue and began to teach.
v.NRSV: They went to Capernaum; and when the sabbath came, he entered the synagogue and taught.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 가버나움으로 들어갔다. 예수께서 안식일에 곧바로 회당에 들어가서 가르치셨는데,
v.WLB: 그들은 가버나움으로 갔습니다. 곧 안식일이 돼 예수께서 회당에 들어가셔서 말씀을 가르치시기 시작하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 大家对他的教训都很惊奇，因为他教导他们，像一个有权柄的人，不像经学家。
v.ESV: And they were astonished at his teaching, for he taught them as one who had authority, and not as the scribes.
v.GAE: 뭇 사람이 그의 교훈에 놀라니 이는 그가 가르치시는 것이 권위 있는 자와 같고 서기관들과 같지 아니함일러라
v.KJV: And they were astonished at his doctrine: for he taught them as one that had authority, and not as the scribes.
v.NASB: They were amazed at His teaching; for He was teaching them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
v.NIV: The people were amazed at his teaching, because he taught them as one who had authority, not as the teachers of the law.
v.NRSV: They were astounded at his teaching, for he taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
v.SAENEW: 사람들은 그의 가르침에 놀랐다. 예수께서 율법학자들과는 달리 권위 있게 가르치셨기 때문이다.
v.WLB: 그러자 사람들은 예수의 가르치심에 놀랐습니다. 율법학자들과 달리, 예수께서는 권위 있는 분처럼 가르치셨기 때문입니다.

v.CNV: 就在那时，会堂里有一个被污灵附着的人，喊叫起来，
v.ESV: And immediately there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit. And he cried out,
v.GAE: 마침 그들의 회당에 더러운 귀신 들린 사람이 있어 소리 질러 이르되
v.KJV: And there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit; and he cried out,
v.NASB: Just then there was a man in their synagogue with an unclean spirit; and he cried out,
v.NIV: Just then a man in their synagogue who was possessed by an impure spirit cried out,
v.NRSV: Just then there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit,
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 회당에 악한 귀신 들린 사람이 하나 있었는데, 그가 큰소리로 이렇게 말하였다.
v.WLB: 바로 그때, 회당 안에 더러운 귀신 들린 사람 한 명이 울부짖었습니다.

v.CNV: 说：“拿撒勒人耶稣，我们跟你有什么关系呢？你来毁灭我们吗？我知道你是谁，你是　神的圣者。”
v.ESV: “What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are— the Holy One of God.”
v.GAE: 나사렛 예수여 우리가 당신과 무슨 상관이 있나이까 우리를 멸하러 왔나이까 나는 당신이 누구인 줄 아노니 하나님의 거룩한 자니이다
v.KJV: Saying, Let us alone; what have we to do with thee, thou Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art, the Holy One of God.
v.NASB: saying, 'What business do we have with each other, Jesus of Nazareth? Have You come to destroy us? I know who You are-- the Holy One of God!'
v.NIV: “What do you want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are—the Holy One of God!”
v.NRSV: and he cried out, “What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are, the Holy One of God.”
v.SAENEW: "나사렛 사람 예수님, 왜 우리를 간섭하려 하십니까? 우리를 없애려고 오셨습니까? 나는 당신이 누구인지 압니다. 하나님께서 보내신 거룩한 분입니다."
v.WLB: “나사렛 예수여! 우리가 당신과 무슨 상관이 있습니까? 우리를 망하게 하려고 오셨습니까? 나는 당신이 누구신 줄 압니다. 하나님께서 보내신 거룩한 분이십니다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣斥责他说：“住口！从他身上出来！”
v.ESV: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be silent, and come out of him!”
v.GAE: 예수께서 꾸짖어 이르시되 잠잠하고 그 사람에게서 나오라 하시니
v.KJV: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him.
v.NASB: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, 'Be quiet, and come out of him!'
v.NIV: “Be quiet!” said Jesus sternly. “Come out of him!”
v.NRSV: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be silent, and come out of him!”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그를 꾸짖어 말씀하셨다. "입을 다물고 이 사람에게서 나가라."
v.WLB: 예수께서 귀신을 꾸짖으시며 말씀하셨습니다. “조용히 하여라. 그리고 그 사람에게서 나와라!”

v.CNV: 污灵使那人抽疯，大声喊叫，就从他身上出来了。
v.ESV: And the unclean spirit, convulsing him and crying out with a loud voice, came out of him.
v.GAE: 더러운 귀신이 그 사람에게 경련을 일으키고 큰 소리를 지르며 나오는지라
v.KJV: And when the unclean spirit had torn him, and cried with a loud voice, he came out of him.
v.NASB: Throwing him into convulsions, the unclean spirit cried out with a loud voice and came out of him.
v.NIV: The impure spirit shook the man violently and came out of him with a shriek.
v.NRSV: And the unclean spirit, convulsing him and crying with a loud voice, came out of him.
v.SAENEW: 그러자 악한 귀신은 그에게 경련을 일으켜 놓고서 큰 소리를 지르며 떠나갔다.
v.WLB: 그러자 더러운 귀신은 그 사람에게 발작을 일으키더니 비명을 지르며 떠나갔습니다.

v.CNV: 众人都很惊讶，于是彼此对问说：“这是怎么一回事？是个有权能的新道理啊！他吩咐污灵，污灵竟服从了他！”
v.ESV: And they were all amazed, so that they questioned among themselves, saying, “What is this? A new teaching with authority! He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him.”
v.GAE: 다 놀라 서로 물어 이르되 이는 어찜이냐 권위 있는 새 교훈이로다 더러운 귀신들에게 명한즉 순종하는도다 하더라
v.KJV: And they were all amazed, insomuch that they questioned among themselves, saying, What thing is this? what new doctrine is this? for with authority commandeth he even the unclean spirits, and they do obey him.
v.NASB: They were all amazed, so that they debated among themselves, saying, 'What is this? A new teaching with authority! He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey Him.'
v.NIV: The people were all so amazed that they asked each other, “What is this? A new teaching—and with authority! He even gives orders to impure spirits and they obey him.”
v.NRSV: They were all amazed, and they kept on asking one another, “What is this? A new teaching — with authority! He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him.”
v.SAENEW: 사람들이 모두 놀라서 "이게 어찌된 일이냐? 권위 있는 새로운 가르침이다! 그가 악한 귀신들에게 명하시니, 그들도 복종하는구나!" 하면서 서로 물었다.
v.WLB: 사람들은 모두 너무나 놀란 나머지 서로 수군거렸습니다. “이게 무슨 일이지? 권위 있는 새로운 가르침이로군. 저가 더러운 귀신에게 명령까지 하고 귀신도 그에게 복종하니 말이야.”

v.CNV: 耶稣的名声立刻传遍了加利利一带。
v.ESV: And at once his fame spread everywhere throughout all the surrounding region of Galilee.
v.GAE: 예수의 소문이 곧 온 갈릴리 사방에 퍼지더라
v.KJV: And immediately his fame spread abroad throughout all the region round about Galilee.
v.NASB: Immediately the news about Him spread everywhere into all the surrounding district of Galilee.
v.NIV: News about him spread quickly over the whole region of Galilee.
v.NRSV: At once his fame began to spread throughout the surrounding region of Galilee.
v.SAENEW: 그리하여 예수의 소문이 곧 갈릴리 주위의 온 지역에 두루 퍼졌다.
v.WLB: 그래서 예수에 대한 소문이 갈릴리 온 지역으로 삽시간에 퍼졌습니다.

v.CNV: 他们一出会堂，就和雅各、约翰到西门和安得烈的家里去。
v.ESV: And immediately he left the synagogue and entered the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
v.GAE: 회당에서 나와 곧 야고보와 요한과 함께 시몬과 안드레의 집에 들어가시니
v.KJV: And forthwith, when they were come out of the synagogue, they entered into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
v.NASB: And immediately after they came out of the synagogue, they came into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
v.NIV: As soon as they left the synagogue, they went with James and John to the home of Simon and Andrew.
v.NRSV: As soon as they left the synagogue, they entered the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 회당에서 나와서, 곧바로 야고보와 요한과 함께 시몬과 안드레의 집으로 갔다.
v.WLB: 그들은 회당에서 나와 곧바로 야고보와 요한과 함께 시몬과 안드레의 집으로 갔습니다.

v.CNV: 西门的岳母正在发烧躺着，他们立刻告诉耶稣。
v.ESV: Now Simon's mother-in-law lay ill with a fever, and immediately they told him about her.
v.GAE: 시몬의 장모가 열병으로 누워 있는지라 사람들이 곧 그 여자에 대하여 예수께 여짜온대
v.KJV: But Simon’s wife’s mother lay sick of a fever, and anon they tell him of her.
v.NASB: Now Simon's mother-in-law was lying sick with a fever; and immediately they spoke to Jesus about her.
v.NIV: Simon’s mother-in-law was in bed with a fever, and they immediately told Jesus about her.
v.NRSV: Now Simon’s mother-in-law was in bed with a fever, and they told him about her at once.
v.SAENEW: 마침 시몬의 장모가 열병으로 누워 있었는데, 사람들은 그 사정을 예수께 말씀드렸다.
v.WLB: 이때 시몬의 장모가 열병으로 앓아누워 있었습니다. 사람들은 즉시 이 사실을 예수께 말씀 드렸습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣走到她面前，拉着她的手，扶她起来，热就退了，她就服事他们。
v.ESV: And he came and took her by the hand and lifted her up, and the fever left her, and she began to serve them.
v.GAE: 나아가사 그 손을 잡아 일으키시니 열병이 떠나고 여자가 그들에게 수종드니라
v.KJV: And he came and took her by the hand, and lifted her up; and immediately the fever left her, and she ministered unto them.
v.NASB: And He came to her and raised her up, taking her by the hand, and the fever left her, and she waited on them.
v.NIV: So he went to her, took her hand and helped her up. The fever left her and she began to wait on them.
v.NRSV: He came and took her by the hand and lifted her up. Then the fever left her, and she began to serve them.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그 여자에게 다가가셔서 그 손을 잡아 일으키시니, 열병이 떠나고, 그 여자는 그들의 시중을 들었다.
v.WLB: 그래서 예수께서 그 여인에게 다가가셔서 손을 잡고 일으키셨습니다. 그러자 그 즉시 시몬 장모의 열이 떨어졌습니다. 곧바로 그 여인은 그들을 시중들기 시작했습니다.

v.CNV: 到了黄昏，有人不断把生病的和被鬼附的，都带到耶稣面前。
v.ESV: That evening at sundown they brought to him all who were sick or oppressed by demons.
v.GAE: 저물어 해 질 때에 모든 병자와 귀신 들린 자를 예수께 데려오니
v.KJV: And at even, when the sun did set, they brought unto him all that were diseased, and them that were possessed with devils.
v.NASB: When evening came, after the sun had set, they began bringing to Him all who were ill and those who were demon-possessed.
v.NIV: That evening after sunset the people brought to Jesus all the sick and demon-possessed.
v.NRSV: That evening, at sunset, they brought to him all who were sick or possessed with demons.
v.SAENEW: 해가 져서 날이 저물 때에, 사람들이 모든 병자와 귀신 들린 사람을 예수께로 데리고 왔다.
v.WLB: 그날 저녁 해진 후에 사람들이 아픈 사람들과 귀신 들린 사람들을 전부 예수께로 데려왔습니다.

v.CNV: 全城的人都聚集在门口。
v.ESV: And the whole city was gathered together at the door.
v.GAE: 온 동네가 그 문 앞에 모였더라
v.KJV: And all the city was gathered together at the door.
v.NASB: And the whole city had gathered at the door.
v.NIV: The whole town gathered at the door,
v.NRSV: And the whole city was gathered around the door.
v.SAENEW: 그리고 온 동네 사람이 문 앞에 모여들었다.
v.WLB: 온 동네 사람들이 문 앞에 모여들었습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣医好了各样的病，也赶出许多的鬼，并且不许鬼说话，因为鬼认识他。
v.ESV: And he healed many who were sick with various diseases, and cast out many demons. And he would not permit the demons to speak, because they knew him.
v.GAE: 예수께서 각종 병이 든 많은 사람을 고치시며 많은 귀신을 내쫓으시되 귀신이 자기를 알므로 그 말하는 것을 허락하지 아니하시니라
v.KJV: And he healed many that were sick of divers diseases, and cast out many devils; and suffered not the devils to speak, because they knew him.
v.NASB: And He healed many who were ill with various diseases, and cast out many demons; and He was not permitting the demons to speak, because they knew who He was.
v.NIV: and Jesus healed many who had various diseases. He also drove out many demons, but he would not let the demons speak because they knew who he was.
v.NRSV: And he cured many who were sick with various diseases, and cast out many demons; and he would not permit the demons to speak, because they knew him.
v.SAENEW: 그는 온갖 병에 걸린 사람들을 고쳐 주시고, 많은 귀신을 내쫓으셨다. 예수께서는 귀신들이 말하는 것을 허락하지 않으셨다. 그들이 예수가 누구인지를 알았기 때문이다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 온갖 병에 걸린 사람들을 많이 고쳐 주셨습니다. 그리고 많은 귀신들도 내쫓아 주셨습니다. 예수께서는 귀신들이 예수가 누구신지 알고 있기 때문에 귀신들이 말하는 것을 허락하지 않으셨습니다.

v.CNV: 次日凌晨，天还没有亮，耶稣起身出去，来到荒野的地方，在那里祷告。
v.ESV: And rising very early in the morning, while it was still dark, he departed and went out to a desolate place, and there he prayed.
v.GAE: 새벽 아직도 밝기 전에 예수께서 일어나 나가 한적한 곳으로 가사 거기서 기도하시더니
v.KJV: And in the morning, rising up a great while before day, he went out, and departed into a solitary place, and there prayed.
v.NASB: In the early morning, while it was still dark, Jesus got up, left the house, and went away to a secluded place, and was praying there.
v.NIV: Very early in the morning, while it was still dark, Jesus got up, left the house and went off to a solitary place, where he prayed.
v.NRSV: In the morning, while it was still very dark, he got up and went out to a deserted place, and there he prayed.
v.SAENEW: 아주 이른 새벽에, 예수께서 일어나서 외딴 곳으로 나가셔서, 거기에서 기도하고 계셨다.
v.WLB: 매우 이른 새벽 아직 어둑어둑할 때 예수께서 일어나 외딴 곳으로 가셔서 기도하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 西门和那些跟他在一起的人就去寻找耶稣。
v.ESV: And Simon and those who were with him searched for him,
v.GAE: 시몬과 및 그와 함께 있는 자들이 예수의 뒤를 따라가
v.KJV: And Simon and they that were with him followed after him.
v.NASB: Simon and his companions searched for Him;
v.NIV: Simon and his companions went to look for him,
v.NRSV: And Simon and his companions hunted for him.
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 시몬과 그의 일행이 예수를 찾아 나섰다.
v.WLB: 시몬과 그 일행들이 예수를 찾아 나섰습니다.

v.CNV: 他们找到了，就对他说：“大家都在找你呢！”
v.ESV: and they found him and said to him, “Everyone is looking for you.”
v.GAE: 만나서 이르되 모든 사람이 주를 찾나이다
v.KJV: And when they had found him, they said unto him, All men seek for thee.
v.NASB: they found Him, and said to Him, 'Everyone is looking for You.'
v.NIV: and when they found him, they exclaimed: “Everyone is looking for you!”
v.NRSV: When they found him, they said to him, “Everyone is searching for you.”
v.SAENEW: 그들은 예수를 만나자 "모두 선생님을 찾고 있습니다" 하고 말하였다.
v.WLB: 그들이 마침내 예수를 만나자 소리쳐 말했습니다. “모든 사람들이 선생님을 찾고 있습니다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣对他们说：“我们到邻近的乡镇去吧，我也好在那里传道，因为我就是为这事而来的。”
v.ESV: And he said to them, “Let us go on to the next towns, that I may preach there also, for that is why I came out.”
v.GAE: 이르시되 우리가 다른 가까운 마을들로 가자 거기서도 전도하리니 내가 이를 위하여 왔노라 하시고
v.KJV: And he said unto them, Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also: for therefore came I forth.
v.NASB: He said to them, 'Let us go somewhere else to the towns nearby, so that I may preach there also; for that is what I came for.'
v.NIV: Jesus replied, “Let us go somewhere else—to the nearby villages—so I can preach there also. That is why I have come.”
v.NRSV: He answered, “Let us go on to the neighboring towns, so that I may proclaim the message there also; for that is what I came out to do.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "가까운 여러 고을로 가자. 거기에서도 내가 말씀을 선포해야 하겠다. 나는 이 일을 하러 왔다."
v.WLB: 예수께서 대답하셨습니다. “가까운 이웃 마을들에 가서도 말씀을 전파하도록 하자. 내가 이 일을 하러 왔다.”

v.CNV: 于是他走遍加利利全地，在他们的会堂里传道，并且赶鬼。
v.ESV: And he went throughout all Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and casting out demons.
v.GAE: 이에 온 갈릴리에 다니시며 그들의 여러 회당에서 전도하시고 또 귀신들을 내쫓으시더라
v.KJV: And he preached in their synagogues throughout all Galilee, and cast out devils.
v.NASB: And He went into their synagogues throughout all Galilee, preaching and casting out the demons.
v.NIV: So he traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and driving out demons.
v.NRSV: And he went throughout Galilee, proclaiming the message in their synagogues and casting out demons.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 온 갈릴리와 여러 회당을 두루 찾아가셔서 말씀을 전하고, 귀신들을 쫓아내셨다.
v.WLB: 그리하여 예수께서는 갈릴리에 두루 다니시며 여러 회당에서 가르치시고 귀신들을 쫓아내셨습니다.

v.CNV: 有一个患痲风的人，来到耶稣跟前，跪下求他说：“如果你肯，必能使我洁净。”
v.ESV: And a leper came to him, imploring him, and kneeling said to him, “If you will, you can make me clean.”
v.GAE: 한 나병환자가 예수께 와서 꿇어 엎드려 간구하여 이르되 원하시면 저를 깨끗하게 하실 수 있나이다
v.KJV: And there came a leper to him, beseeching him, and kneeling down to him, and saying unto him, If thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
v.NASB: And a leper came to Jesus, beseeching Him and falling on his knees before Him, and saying, 'If You are willing, You can make me clean.'
v.NIV: A man with leprosy came to him and begged him on his knees, “If you are willing, you can make me clean.”
v.NRSV: A leper came to him begging him, and kneeling he said to him, “If you choose, you can make me clean.”
v.SAENEW: 나병 환자 한 사람이 예수께로 와서, 그 앞에 무릎을 꿇고 간청하였다. "선생님께서 하고자 하시면, 나를 깨끗하게 해주실 수 있습니다."
v.WLB: 어떤 나병 환자가 예수께 다가와 무릎을 꿇고 애원했습니다. “선생님께서 원하시기만 하면 저를 깨끗하게 해 주실 수 있습니다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣动了怜悯的心，就伸手摸他，说：“我肯，你洁净了吧！”
v.ESV: Moved with pity, he stretched out his hand and touched him and said to him, “I will; be clean.”
v.GAE: 예수께서 불쌍히 여기사 손을 내밀어 그에게 대시며 이르시되 내가 원하노니 깨끗함을 받으라 하시니
v.KJV: And Jesus, moved with compassion, put forth his hand, and touched him, and saith unto him, I will; be thou clean.
v.NASB: Moved with compassion, Jesus stretched out His hand and touched him, and said to him, 'I am willing; be cleansed.'
v.NIV: Jesus was indignant. He reached out his hand and touched the man. “I am willing,” he said. “Be clean!”
v.NRSV: Moved with pity, Jesus stretched out his hand and touched him, and said to him, “I do choose. Be made clean!”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그를 불쌍히 여기시고, 손을 내밀어 그에게 대시고 말씀하셨다. "그렇게 해주마. 깨끗하게 되어라."
v.WLB: 예수께서 불쌍히 여기시고 손을 내밀어 그를 만지시며 말씀하셨습니다. “내가 원한다. 자, 깨끗이 나아라!”

v.CNV: 痲风立刻离开了他，他就洁净了。
v.ESV: And immediately the leprosy left him, and he was made clean.
v.GAE: 곧 나병이 그 사람에게서 떠나가고 깨끗하여진지라
v.KJV: And as soon as he had spoken, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was cleansed.
v.NASB: Immediately the leprosy left him and he was cleansed.
v.NIV: Immediately the leprosy left him and he was cleansed.
v.NRSV: Immediately the leprosy left him, and he was made clean.
v.SAENEW: 곧 나병이 그에게서 떠나고, 그는 깨끗하게 되었다.
v.WLB: 그러자 나병이 순식간에 사라지고 그가 깨끗이 나았습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣打发那人离开以前，严厉地吩咐他，
v.ESV: And Jesus sternly charged him and sent him away at once,
v.GAE: 곧 보내시며 엄히 경고하사
v.KJV: And he straitly charged him, and forthwith sent him away;
v.NASB: And He sternly warned him and immediately sent him away,
v.NIV: Jesus sent him away at once with a strong warning:
v.NRSV: After sternly warning him he sent him away at once,
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 단단히 이르시고, 곧 그를 보내셨다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 곧바로 그를 보내시며 단단히 당부하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 说：“你千万不可把这事告诉任何人，你只要去给祭司检查，并且照着摩西所规定的，为你得洁净献祭，好向大家作证。”
v.ESV: and said to him, “See that you say nothing to anyone, but go, show yourself to the priest and offer for your cleansing what Moses commanded, for a proof to them.”
v.GAE: 이르시되 삼가 아무에게 아무 말도 하지 말고 가서 네 몸을 제사장에게 보이고 네가 깨끗하게 되었으니 모세가 명한 것을 드려 그들에게 입증하라 하셨더라
v.KJV: And saith unto him, See thou say nothing to any man: but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing those things which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
v.NASB: and He said to him, 'See that you say nothing to anyone; but go, show yourself to the priest and offer for your cleansing what Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.'
v.NIV: “See that you don’t tell this to anyone. But go, show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifices that Moses commanded for your cleansing, as a testimony to them.”
v.NRSV: saying to him, “See that you say nothing to anyone; but go, show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing what Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.”
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 예수께서 그에게 말씀하셨다. "아무에게도 아무 말도 하지 말아라. 가서, 제사장에게 네 몸을 보이고, 네가 깨끗하게 된 것에 대하여 모세가 명령한 것을 바쳐서, 사람들에게 증거로 삼도록 하여라."
v.WLB: “이 일에 대해 아무에게도 아무 말도 하지 마라. 다만 제사장에게 가서 네 몸을 보이고 네가 깨끗이 나은 것에 대해 모세가 명령한 대로 예물을 드려 사람들에게 증거를 삼아라.”

v.CNV: 但那人出来，竟任意传讲，就把这事传开了，以致耶稣不能再公开进城，只好留在外边荒野的地方；然而还是有人从各处到他那里去。
v.ESV: But he went out and began to talk freely about it, and to spread the news, so that Jesus could no longer openly enter a town, but was out in desolate places, and people were coming to him from every quarter.
v.GAE: 그러나 그 사람이 나가서 이 일을 많이 전파하여 널리 퍼지게 하니 그러므로 예수께서 다시는 드러나게 동네에 들어가지 못하시고 오직 바깥 한적한 곳에 계셨으나 사방에서 사람들이 그에게로 나아오더라
v.KJV: But he went out, and began to publish it much, and to blaze abroad the matter, insomuch that Jesus could no more openly enter into the city, but was without in desert places: and they came to him from every quarter.
v.NASB: But he went out and began to proclaim it freely and to spread the news around, to such an extent that Jesus could no longer publicly enter a city, but stayed out in unpopulated areas; and they were coming to Him from everywhere.
v.NIV: Instead he went out and began to talk freely, spreading the news. As a result, Jesus could no longer enter a town openly but stayed outside in lonely places. Yet the people still came to him from everywhere.
v.NRSV: But he went out and began to proclaim it freely, and to spread the word, so that Jesus could no longer go into a town openly, but stayed out in the country; and people came to him from every quarter.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 그는 나가서, 모든 일을 널리 알리고, 그 이야기를 퍼뜨렸다. 그러므로 예수께서는 드러나게 동네로 들어가지 못하시고, 바깥 외딴 곳에 머물러 계셨다. 그래도 사람들이 사방에서 예수께로 모여들었다.
v.WLB: 그러나 그 사람은 나가서 이 일을 마구 널리 퍼뜨렸습니다. 그 결과 예수께서는 더 이상 드러나게 마을 안으로 들어가지 못하고 마을 밖 외딴 곳에 머물러 계셨습니다. 그래도 사람들은 여전히 사방에서 예수께로 모여들었습니다.
